description: Make the dev reload relay server robust to port conflicts by trying a range of ports, starting the server before extension builds, and passing the actual bound port to build scripts via environment variable. This also eliminates the hardcoded port duplication across files.

userStories.title: Make relay server try a range of ports and return the actual bound port
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the dev reload relay server to automatically find an available port so that dev mode works even when the preferred port is occupied.
acceptanceCriteria: startDevReloadServer accepts a starting port and tries up to 10 sequential ports (e.g., 18515-18524)
The returned DevReloadServer interface includes a `port` field with the actual bound port
If none of the 10 ports are available, returns null (same as today)
The server logs which port it actually bound to
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes

userStories.title: Reorder dev startup to start relay server before extension builds
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the relay server to start before extension builds so that the actual bound port is known at build time and can be baked into the client scripts.
acceptanceCriteria: The dev relay server starts immediately after tsc initial compilation completes, BEFORE the extension build pipeline
The actual bound port is set as OPENTABS_DEV_RELOAD_PORT environment variable before any extension build runs
Both build-side-panel.ts and build-extension.ts read the port from process.env.OPENTABS_DEV_RELOAD_PORT
If the relay server failed to start (null), OPENTABS_DEV_RELOAD_PORT is not set and build scripts skip dev reload client injection
The startup banner displays the actual bound port (not the preferred port)
The hardcoded '18515' strings in build-side-panel.ts and build-extension.ts are removed
npm run build passes (production build — no env vars set, no injection)
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
